title: Protecting Americans from Tax Hikes on Imported Goods Act of 2025
short_title: Protecting Americans from Tax Hikes on Imported Goods Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill stops the President from using emergency powers to raise taxes on goods brought into America from other countries. It also prevents the President from setting special limits on how much of certain products can be imported. However, during an emergency, the President can still completely block all imports from specific countries if needed. The law is meant to protect Americans from higher prices on imported goods during emergencies.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs.
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Protecting Americans from Tax Hikes on Imported Goods Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Exclusion of Authority to Impose Duties and Tariff-Rate Quotas from International Emergency Economic Powers Act
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the International Emergency Economic Powers Act. The changes work like this. First, it moves what used to be called part c to become part d of that law. Second, it adds a new part c with two rules. Rule one says that the President cannot use emergency powers to add new taxes on goods coming into America or raise taxes that already exist on those goods. The President also cannot use emergency powers to set limits on how much of certain goods can come in at lower tax rates, which is called a tariff-rate quota. Rule two says that even though the President cannot raise taxes on imports during emergencies, the President can still completely stop all goods or certain types of goods from a specific country from entering the United States.
